Operation 'createFetcher' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too minimal and lacks domain context. AI agents need to understand that fetchers are for importing external email, what inputs are required (FetcherCreate schema), what is returned, and what operational effect this has (polling begins). Enhanced summary and description provide this context using appropriate mail server terminology.

data.summary_suggestion: Create a new email fetcher configuration
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new email fetcher that retrieves messages from an external email account and imports them into a Mailu user account. Requires a FetcherCreate request body with source email credentials and destination user configuration. Returns the created fetcher object with assigned ID and current status. The fetcher will begin polling the external account according to configured intervals.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal and lacks domain context. AI agents need to understand that fetchers are for importing external email, what inputs are required (FetcherCreate schema), what is returned, and what operational effect this has (polling begins). Enhanced summary and description provide this context using appropriate mail server terminology.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve email fetcher configuration and status
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a specific email fetcher by ID, including its configuration, current status, last sync timestamp, and error information if applicable. Requires the fetcher ID as a path parameter. Returns the complete Fetcher object with all configuration and operational details.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what details are returned and what the ID parameter represents. Enhanced version clarifies the path parameter requirement, explains what information is included in the response (configuration, status, sync info, errors), and uses domain-appropriate terminology for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'updateFetcher' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description provides no context about what can be updated, how partial updates work, or what side effects occur. Enhanced version explains the PATCH semantics, what FetcherUpdate contains, that updates are partial, what is returned, and importantly, that configuration changes take effect on next poll cycle for AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing email fetcher's configuration using a PATCH request. Accepts a FetcherUpdate request body with fields to modify (e.g., credentials, polling interval, enabled status). Supports partial updates - only provided fields are modified. Returns the updated Fetcher object. Changes take effect immediately; if the fetcher is enabled, it will use the new configuration on the next polling cycle.
data.reasoning: Original description provides no context about what can be updated, how partial updates work, or what side effects occur. Enhanced version explains the PATCH semantics, what FetcherUpdate contains, that updates are partial, what is returned, and importantly, that configuration changes take effect on next poll cycle for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'deleteFetcher' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal and lacks critical information about side effects and permanence. Enhanced version clarifies the path parameter, explains what happens when deleted (polling stops, pending operations cancelled), indicates the response status, and emphasizes irreversibility - all important for AI agents to understand the consequences of this destructive operation.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes an email fetcher by ID, stopping any active polling of the external email account. Requires the fetcher ID as a path parameter. Returns HTTP 200 on successful deletion. Side effects: the fetcher stops retrieving messages immediately, and any pending sync operations are cancelled. The fetcher configuration cannot be recovered after deletion.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal and lacks critical information about side effects and permanence. Enhanced version clarifies the path parameter, explains what happens when deleted (polling stops, pending operations cancelled), indicates the response status, and emphasizes irreversibility - all important for AI agents to understand the consequences of this destructive operation.
